q.en: What is the biggest part of an orchestra?
q.zh: 管弦乐队中最大的部分是什么？

explain.en: The book says, 'The biggest part of an orchestra is the string section... The string section of a large orchestra can contain over seventy musicians.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“管弦乐队中最大的部分是弦乐声部……大型管弦乐队的弦乐声部可包含七十多名乐手。”

options.en: A group of instruments including flutes, clarinets, oboes, and bassoons
options.zh: 包括长笛、单簧管、双簧管和大管的一组乐器
像小号那样的金属乐器

options.en: Instruments with many keys like a piano
options.zh: 像钢琴那样有许多琴键的乐器

explain.en: The glossary defines woodwinds as 'a group of instruments that includes flutes, clarinets, oboes, and bassoons (p. 11).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 woodwinds 定义为“包括长笛、单簧管、双簧管和大管的一组乐器（第11页）”。

q.zh: 木管乐器是如何演奏的？

options.en: By pressing keys on a piano

explain.en: The book says, 'Woodwind instruments are played by blowing air into them.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“木管乐器是通过向里面吹气来演奏的。”

q.en: Besides the four families in an orchestra, what are four ways instruments around the world are played?
q.zh: 除了管弦乐队的四个组别，世界各地的乐器有哪四种演奏方式？

explain.en: The book states most instruments 'are played in one of four ways—by blowing, scraping, plucking, or hammering.'
explain.zh: 书中说，大多数乐器“以四种方式之一演奏——吹、刮、拨或敲”。

q.en: What main idea does the author want readers to learn about music?
q.zh: 关于音乐，作者想让读者了解的主要观点是什么？

options.en: Music brings joy and is made with voices or instruments in many forms
options.zh: 音乐带来快乐，并以多种形式用嗓音或乐器创造
只有专业人士才应该做音乐

options.en: Music is only for listening, not playing
options.zh: 音乐只用来听，不用来演奏

options.en: Instruments are too hard to learn

explain.en: The book concludes, 'Music brings great joy to life... Whether you sing, dance, play an instrument, or sit and listen, be sure to make it a part of your life.'
explain.zh: 书中总结道：“音乐为生活带来巨大的快乐……无论你唱歌、跳舞、演奏乐器还是静坐聆听，都要让它成为你生活的一部分。”

q.en: What instrument in an orchestra is plucked instead of bowed?
q.zh: 管弦乐队中哪种乐器是拨奏而非用弓拉的？

explain.en: The book says, 'Another stringed instrument in an orchestra is plucked instead of bowed. It is the harp.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“管弦乐队中另一种弦乐器是拨奏而非用弓拉的，那就是竖琴。”

options.en: A metal instrument such as a trumpet, trombone, horn, or tuba
options.zh: 如小号、长号、圆号或大号之类的金属乐器

options.en: An instrument you blow into that has holes

explain.en: The glossary defines brass as 'a group of metal instruments that includes trumpets, trombones, horns, and tubas (p. 13).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 brass 定义为“一组金属乐器，包括小号、长号、圆号和大号（第13页）”。

q.en: What extra percussion instrument do Latin bands often have?
q.zh: 拉丁乐队通常会有哪种额外的打击乐器？

explain.en: The book says, 'Latin bands often have extra percussion such as the marimba, an instrument related to a xylophone.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“拉丁乐队通常会有额外的打击乐器，例如马林巴琴，一种与木琴相关的乐器。”

q.en: Which instrument does the book say can be considered a musical instrument?
q.zh: 书中说以下哪一种可以被视为一种乐器？

explain.en: The book states, 'In many ways, the human voice can be considered a musical instrument. It is perhaps the most flexible musical instrument of all.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“在很多方面，人声可以被视为一种乐器。它也许是最灵活的一种乐器。”

q.en: Why does the author give examples of instruments from many countries?
q.zh: 作者为什么要列举来自许多国家的乐器例子？

options.en: To show that cultures worldwide have different but related instruments
options.zh: 为了展示世界各地的文化有不同但相关的乐器

options.en: To prove only Europe has good music
options.zh: 为了证明只有欧洲有好音乐

options.en: To list which country invented the piano
options.zh: 为了列出哪个国家发明了钢琴

explain.en: The 'Instruments Around the World' section shows scraped, plucked, and hammered instruments from places like Africa, Europe, India, and Japan.
explain.zh: “世界各地的乐器”一节展示了来自非洲、欧洲、印度、日本等地的刮奏、拨奏和敲击乐器。

prompt.en: Think about an instrument you would like to learn. Which family does it belong to, and how is it played? Use the book's ideas.
prompt.zh: 想想你想学的一种乐器。它属于哪个类别，又是如何演奏的？运用书中的观点。

en: Mention one of the four ways instruments are played (blow, scrape, pluck, hammer) or one of the families.
Name a specific instrument from the book.
zh: 提到乐器四种演奏方式之一（吹、刮、拨、敲）或某个类别。
说出书中一种具体的乐器。
